title: Hello world — starting the lab notebook
description: A first post about why this site exists and what it'll cover going forward.
bodyMd:
## Why this exists

I've spent the last six years building software for other people — first at Lipsey's, then at Tri-Core — while shipping my own projects on the side. *CookJunkie*, *TutorTab*, *Vorbiz*, and *Weekend Plant* all started because I wanted to see what I could build on my own, end to end.

This blog is the notebook where I write about that work.

## What I'll write about

- **Bringing old systems forward.** Taking software that still works but is starting to show its age, and modernizing it without the business skipping a beat.
- **Using AI to do real work.** The kind that reviews data, drafts content, and handles the repetitive tasks that used to eat my afternoons, rather than chatbot demos.
- **Building and running software on my own.** The unglamorous parts: payments that go sideways, calendars that silently drop appointments, and customers who need the app to work on a dead connection.
- **Short notes on things I learn.** Practical and specific, not marketing filler.

## What to expect

Posts may be written, short videos, or both together — whichever format fits the topic.

## What I'm working on right now

This site. The portfolio and the blog both live here, and getting them usable was the first step.

If any of this sounds like the kind of work you need, there's a [contact form](/contact). Tell me what you're working on.
